title: Airspace reopens — Iran, Syria (Damascus International Airport), Iraq (after 72-hour closure); EASA advisory valid until June 10
snippet: Iran Civil Aviation Organization announced Iranian airspace returns to 'normal conditions' following the mutual halt of hostilities. Syria: Damascus International Airport resumed operations. Iraq: airspace reopened after a 72-hour closure. EASA advisory remains valid until June 10, indicating some residual caution. This reopening signals de-escalation in the aviation sector across the Middle East. Covered by Gulf News, Wego Travel Blog, Crystal Travel.
tag_reason: First Middle East airspace reopening after the June 7-8 escalation — new event signaling de-escalation; covers three countries simultaneously
